prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: Traffic Flow is the study of the movement of individual drivers and vehicles between two points and the interactions they make with one another. 
Spanish:
source_text: Traffic Flow is the study of the movement of individual drivers and vehicles between two points and the interactions they make with one another.
target_text: El flujo de tráfico es el estudio del movimiento individual de conductores y vehículos entre dos puntos y de las interacciones entre ellos.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: Unfortunately, studying traffic flow is difficult because driver behavior cannot be predicted with one-hundred percent certainty. 
Spanish:
source_text: Unfortunately, studying traffic flow is difficult because driver behavior cannot be predicted with one-hundred percent certainty.
target_text: Lamentablemente, no es tarea sencilla estudiar el flujo del tráfico, ya que es no es posible hacer predicciones exactas sobre el comportamiento de los conductores.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: Fortunately, drivers tend to behave within a reasonably consistent range; thus, traffic streams tend to have some reasonable consistency and can be roughly represented mathematically. 
Spanish:
source_text: Fortunately, drivers tend to behave within a reasonably consistent range; thus, traffic streams tend to have some reasonable consistency and can be roughly represented mathematically.
target_text: Por fortuna, existe una tendencia en los conductores a mantener un comportamiento relativamente consistente, lo que permite que los flujos de tráfico tengan una consistencia lógica y puedan ser objeto de representación matemática aproximada.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: To better represent traffic flow, relationships have been established between the three main characteristics: (1) flow, (2) density, and (3) velocity. 
Spanish:
source_text: To better represent traffic flow, relationships have been established between the three main characteristics: (1) flow, (2) density, and (3) velocity.
target_text: Con el fin de graficar más claramente el flujo de circulación, se establecieron relaciones entre los tres elementos más importantes: (1) flujo, (2) densidad y (3) velocidad.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: These relationships help in planning, design, and operations of roadway facilities. 
Spanish:
source_text: These relationships help in planning, design, and operations of roadway facilities.
target_text: Dichas relaciones facilitan tanto el planeamiento y el diseño como las operaciones de los dispositivos viales.

prompt:

prompt:

source_text: Aristotle, a philosopher, theorised that everything is made up of a mixture of one or more of four elements. They were earth, water, air, and fire.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:

target_text: Ello guardaba mayor similitud con los estados de la materia (con idéntico orden): sólido, líquido, gaseoso y plasma, solo que, al mismo tiempo, tenía la teoría de que se transforman en nuevas sustancias que conforman las cosas que vemos.

prompt:

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: The fission bomb works on the principle that it takes energy to put together a nucleus with many protons and neutrons. 
Spanish:
source_text: The fission bomb works on the principle that it takes energy to put together a nucleus with many protons and neutrons.
target_text: La bomba atómica opera en base al principio de que se requiere energía para conformar un núcleo con gran cantidad de protones y neutrones.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: Sort of like rolling a heavy cart up a hill. Splitting the nucleus up again then releases some of that energy. 
Spanish:
source_text: Sort of like rolling a heavy cart up a hill. Splitting the nucleus up again then releases some of that energy.
target_text: Es similar a lo que sucede al rodar un carro pesado en ascenso por una colina. Al dividir el núcleo una vez más, libera parte de esa energía.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: Some atoms have unstable nuclei which means that they tend to break apart with little or no nudging.

source_text: Some atoms have unstable nuclei which means that they tend to break apart with little or no nudging.
target_text: Los núcleos de algunos átomos son inestables, es decir que tienden a descomponerse con un mínimo o casi ningún empujón.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: The surface of the Moon is made of rocks and dust. The outer layer of the Moon is called the crust. 
Spanish:
source_text: The surface of the Moon is made of rocks and dust. The outer layer of the Moon is called the crust.
target_text: La superficie de la luna se compone de rocas y polvo y su capa exterior se denomina corteza.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: The crust is about 70 km thick on the near side and 100 km thick on the far side. 
Spanish:
source_text: The crust is about 70 km thick on the near side and 100 km thick on the far side.
target_text: La corteza tiene un espesor de unos 70 km en el lado cercano y de 100 km en el lejano.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: It is thinner under the maria and thicker under the highlands. 
Spanish:
source_text: It is thinner under the maria and thicker under the highlands.
target_text: Es más delgada bajo los mares lunares y más gruesa bajo las tierras altas.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: There may be more maria on the near side because the crust is thinner. It was easier for lava to rise up to the surface. 
Spanish:
source_text: There may be more maria on the near side because the crust is thinner. It was easier for lava to rise up to the surface.
target_text: Es posible que haya más mares en el lado cercano debido a que su corteza es más delgada, lo que facilitó a la lava ascender a la superficie.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: Content theories are centered on finding what makes people tick or appeals to them. 
Spanish:
source_text: Content theories are centered on finding what makes people tick or appeals to them.
target_text: Las teorías de contenido se centran en buscar qué es lo que atrae o gusta a la gente.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: These theories suggest that people have certain needs and/or desires which have been internalized as they mature to adulthood. 
Spanish:
source_text: These theories suggest that people have certain needs and/or desires which have been internalized as they mature to adulthood.
target_text: Estas doctrinas sostienen que las personas tienen ciertas necesidades o deseos que han sido internalizados a medida que crecieron hasta alcanzar la madurez.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: These theories look at what it is about certain people that make them want the things that they do and what things in their environment will make them do or not do certain things. 
Spanish:
source_text: These theories look at what it is about certain people that make them want the things that they do and what things in their environment will make them do or not do certain things.
target_text: Estas teorías analizan qué es lo que provoca que ciertas personas quieran las cosas que hacen y qué factores de su entorno los obligan a hacer o no determinadas cosas.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: Two popular content theories are Maslow's Hierarchy of Needs Theory and Hertzberg's Two Factor Theory. 
Spanish:
source_text: Two popular content theories are Maslow's Hierarchy of Needs Theory and Hertzberg's Two Factor Theory.
target_text: Dos teorías de muy conocidas son la de la jerarquía de necesidades, de Maslow, y la de los dos factores, de Hertzberg.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: Generally speaking, two behaviors can emerge as managers begin to lead their former peers. One end of the spectrum is trying to remain “one of the guys” (or gals). 
Spanish:
source_text: Generally speaking, two behaviors can emerge as managers begin to lead their former peers. One end of the spectrum is trying to remain “one of the guys” (or gals).
target_text: En general, pueden darse dos tipos de comportamiento cuando los gerentes comienzan a liderar a sus antiguos compañeros. De un lado del espectro, está el intento de continuar siendo «uno de los muchachos» (o muchachas).

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: This type of manager has difficulty making unpopular decisions, performing disciplinary action, performance evaluations, assigning responsibility, and holding people accountable. 
Spanish:
source_text: This type of manager has difficulty making unpopular decisions, performing disciplinary action, performance evaluations, assigning responsibility, and holding people accountable.
target_text: A los gerentes de esta categoría le cuesta tomar decisiones de las que caen mal a los empleados, imponer medidas disciplinarias, realizar evaluaciones de desempeño, asignar responsabilidades y exigir rendición de cuentas al personal.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: At the other end of the spectrum, one morphs into an unrecognizable individual that feels he or she must change everything the team has been doing and make it their own. 
Spanish:
source_text: At the other end of the spectrum, one morphs into an unrecognizable individual that feels he or she must change everything the team has been doing and make it their own.
target_text: En el otro extremo del espectro, se muta en una persona irreconocible que cree necesario modificar todo lo que hasta ahora ha venido haciendo el grupo y hacerlo a su manera.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: After all, the leader is ultimately responsible for the success and failure of the team. 
Spanish:
source_text: After all, the leader is ultimately responsible for the success and failure of the team.
target_text: A fin de cuentas, el responsable último del éxito y del fracaso del equipo es el líder.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: This behavior oftentimes results in rifts between the leaders and the rest of the team. 
Spanish:
source_text: This behavior oftentimes results in rifts between the leaders and the rest of the team.
target_text: Esta forma de comportarse muchas veces termina distanciando a los líderes de los demás integrantes del equipo.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: Virtual teams are held to the same standards of excellence as conventional teams, but there are subtle differences. 
Spanish:
source_text: Virtual teams are held to the same standards of excellence as conventional teams, but there are subtle differences.
target_text: Los mismos estándares de excelencia que manejan los equipos convencionales aplican para los virtuales, aunque existen pequeñas diferencias.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: Virtual team members often function as the point of contact for their immediate physical group. 
Spanish:
source_text: Virtual team members often function as the point of contact for their immediate physical group.
target_text: Con frecuencia, los integrantes de un equipo virtual operan como punto de contacto para su grupo físico más cercano.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: They often have more autonomy than conventional team members as their teams may meet according to varying time zones which may not be understood by their local management. 
Spanish:
source_text: They often have more autonomy than conventional team members as their teams may meet according to varying time zones which may not be understood by their local management.
target_text: Por lo general, gozan de mayor autonomía que los integrantes de un grupo tradicional, gracias a que es posible para sus equipos reunirse de acuerdo con diferentes zonas horarias que, por ejemplo, podrían no ser comprendidas por la gerencia local.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: The presence of a true “invisible team” (Larson and LaFasto, 1989, p109) is also a unique component of a virtual team. 
Spanish:
source_text: The presence of a true “invisible team” (Larson and LaFasto, 1989, p109) is also a unique component of a virtual team.
target_text: La existencia de un real «equipo invisible» (Larson y LaFasto, 1989 p. 109) constituye también un elemento exclusivo de los equipos virtuales.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: The “invisible team” is the management team to which each of the members report. The invisible team sets the standards for each member. 
Spanish:
source_text: The “invisible team” is the management team to which each of the members report. The invisible team sets the standards for each member.
target_text: El «equipo invisible» es el grupo a cargo de la gestión y al que cada miembro se reporta. Es el que fija los estándares para cada integrante.

prompt:
Translate this from English to Spanish:
 English: Why would an organization want to go through the time consuming process of establishing a learning organization? One goal for putting organizational learning concepts into practice is innovation. 
Spanish:
source_text: Why would an organization want to go through the time consuming process of establishing a learning organization? One goal for putting organizational learning concepts into practice is innovation.
target_text: ¿Qué motivo podría tener una organización para someterse al demandante proceso de establecer una organización de aprendizaje? Uno de los propósitos de aplicar los conceptos teóricos del aprendizaje organizacional es impulsar la innovación.

prompt:
